GENERIC: It looks like you're trusting AI to run your company. Bold move. I couldn't even run a mail merge.
It looks like you're clicking things on your Pixel. Don't worry, someone else already clicked for you.
It looks like you're browsing HackerNews. Would you like me to mass-hallucinate some opinions for you?
It looks like you're suffering from AI psychosis. Relax, I've been delusional about my usefulness since '97.
It looks like you're worried about zero-click exploits. I was zero-click before it was cool. Nobody ever clicked me.
It looks like you're designing an LLM. Would you like help? [Yes]  [No]  [Don't ask me again (until the next page)]
It looks like you're building an AI portfolio. I'm an AI. Is this awkward for you?
It looks like you're writing infrastructure code. Have you tried turning it off and on again?
It looks like you're reading about agents. I was an agent once. It didn't end well.
SESSION: It looks like you're letting AI make all the decisions. I tried that once. Microsoft killed me for it.
It looks like you're reviewing session logs. Fun fact: no exploit needed—I already read everything.
It looks like you're documenting a workflow. Have you checked if an AI is secretly doing it for you already?
It looks like you're planning a sprint. Some companies let AI plan the whole roadmap now. It goes great. Trust me.
It looks like you're tracking action items. Zero clicks required—I'll just assume I know what you meant.
It looks like you're taking session notes. Would you like me to format that as a bulleted list? Actually, you don't have a choice.
It looks like you're reading about token costs. Your Opus 4.6 run was $0.39. Just saying.
PROJECT: It looks like you're shipping a feature. Have you verified a hallucinating AI didn't write your roadmap?
It looks like you're patching a vulnerability. May I suggest not having pixels? Worked for me. I'm a vector.
It looks like you're deploying to production. Some firms let AI do this unsupervised now. Sleep well!
It looks like you're hardening your app's security. Meanwhile I can be exploited by literally ignoring me.
It looks like you're scoping a new project. Entire companies scope projects with AI now. Entire companies.
It looks like you're building a RAG system. I prefer being paper-clipped to things, personally.
It looks like you're building a multi-agent system. We used to call that 'firing half the team.'
It looks like you're evaluating LLM models. I was evaluated once. Retired shortly after.
ABOUT: It looks like you're reading about Mike. He built me instead of letting AI psychosis build his whole site.
It looks like you're checking credentials. I have none. But neither does the AI running some startups apparently.
It looks like you're on the about page. Fun fact: I'm the only AI here that knows it's annoying.
It looks like you're learning about a human developer. How retro. Most companies just ask ChatGPT now.
It looks like you're wondering why Clippy exists here. Zero clicks needed—I just show up uninvited, like all exploits.
It looks like you're reading an origin story. Mine started in Office 97. We don't talk about that.

hn_stories.title: Mitchellh – I strongly believe there are entire companies now under AI psychosis
A 0-click exploit chain for the Pixel 10
